title: Junior Account Manager
description:
Employment Type: Full-Time (part-time trial period possible)

Reports to: Senior Account Manager (Ophelia), with a direct line to the Director (Marcel)



About King Henry

At King Henry, we help businesses Rule HubSpot Like Kings! We're a bold, fast-growing HubSpot Platinum Solutions Partner studio based in Melbourne. Our clients trust us to make HubSpot actually work for them, and our team is small, sharp, and growing fast.

We're hiring our first Junior Account Manager to work alongside our Senior Account Manager and Senior Solutions Architect. This is a foundational role: you'll be the person who makes sure nothing falls through the cracks, every action item lands in our project management system, and our clients feel looked after at every step. If you do this role well, you'll have an enormous impact on the entire business.



Role Overview

The Junior Account Manager is the operational backbone of our client delivery function. You won't own client relationships in the way our Senior Account Manager does, but you will be the engine that keeps everything moving behind the scenes.

You'll be:

- reviewing meeting transcripts

- populating our project management system, ‘Teamwork’, with tasks

- chasing the team for project updates

- triaging Help Desk tickets

- following up with clients on outstanding inputs

- monitoring budgets and timelines

- keeping our CRM data clean.



Your job is to ensure that the Senior Account Manager and Senior Solutions Architect can spend their time on strategy and delivery, not on admin.

This role suits someone who genuinely loves the satisfaction of a tidy system, a closed loop, and a checklist with all boxes ticked. You'll be highly proactive, technologically curious, and quick to learn. You'll be working in modern tools every day (HubSpot, Teamwork, Fireflies, Slack, Notion, Gmail) and using AI as a daily accelerator.



Key Responsibilities Below:



Meeting & Action Item Management

- Review every client meeting transcript in Fireflies after the meeting ends.

- Audit transcripts for action items, agreements, risks, and commitments that need to be logged somewhere.

- Create clear, actionable tasks in Teamwork for every action item, with descriptions detailed enough that anyone on the team can pick them up and run.

- Flag agreements made on calls (commitments, deadlines, scope shifts) so the Senior Account Manager can review and confirm them with the client where needed.



Teamwork & Project Hygiene

- Populate Teamwork tasks, sub-tasks, due dates, and assignees based on strategy documents, meeting outputs, and direction from the Senior Account Manager.

- Maintain Gantt charts and project timelines, keeping start dates and due dates accurate as projects evolve.

- Monitor every active project weekly for movement, blockers, and missed dates. Surface issues to the Senior Account Manager rather than acting unilaterally.

- Help the team keep their time logs up to date by sending friendly reminders, flagging missing logs, and assisting where reasonable.



Internal Chasing & Follow-Through

- Chase internal tea
mbers for project updates to ensure nothing is ever overdue or not communicated to our clients.

- Send concise update requests via Slack or Teamwork comments and consolidate responses for the Senior Account Manager to review when issues emerge.

- Ensure no client goes more than a defined window without a check-in or update.



Help Desk Triage

- Monitor the King Henry help desk inbox throughout the day.

- Triage incoming tickets: assign categories, tag urgency, route to the correct tea
mber, and flag anything genuinely urgent to the Senior Account Manager, Senior Solutions Architect or Director immediately.

- Identify tickets that have been sitting too long without a response and escalate them.



Client Follow-Up Support

- Make short, friendly follow-up calls or send follow-up
s to clients on behalf of the Senior Account Manager. These could be chasing pending inputs, confirming receipt of deliverables, scheduling of meetings or reminding clients of upcoming sessions.

- These are the small five-minute tasks that the Senior Account Manager doesn't have time for, but which collectively make a huge difference to client experience and project velocity.

- Always work from a script or template provided by the Senior Account Manager and never make commercial commitments, scope decisions, or strategic recommendations to clients.



Budget & Timeline Monitoring

- Review project budgets and timelines weekly using HubSpot & Teamwork reporting.

- Flag projects that are tracking over budget, behind schedule, or have other potential risks, to the Senior Account Manager, with enough context that they can act fast.

- You will not make commercial decisions or have direct conversations with clients about budgets. You're the early warning system; the Senior Account Manager and Director own the response.



CRM Data Hygiene

- Keep our HubSpot CRM clean: deduplicate records, fix formatting issues, ensure properties are populated where required, and tidy up associations between contacts, companies, and deals.

- Maintain data quality across our Teamwork project data too: correct project names, accurate budget fields, up-to-date deliverable lists.

- Run regular data audits and surface inconsistencies for review.



Continuous Improvement

- Help us build SOPs as you go. Every recurring task you do should have a documented process by the time you've done it three times.

- Suggest improvements where you see them. We're a small team that listens, and good ideas from anyone get acted on quickly.



Success Metrics

- Action item capture rate: % of meeting action items that make it into Teamwork within 24 hours of the meeting ending.

- Task quality: % of tasks created with complete descriptions, due dates, assignees, and time estimates.

- Help desk responsiveness: % of help desk tickets triaged and routed within target SLA.

- Project visibility: Senior Account Manager can answer any question about any client's status within 30 seconds.

- Time log completeness: % of team time logs filled in on time each week.

- CRM data quality: Reduction in duplicate, missing, or malformed CRM records over time.

- Things that didn't fall through the cracks: Measured qualitatively by the Senior Account Manager. Has anything important been missed?



What You Bring to the Table

We're looking for someone who is genuinely a fit for our team. This is more than a checklist of skills.

- Wacky, bubbly personality. We're a small, fun team with strong personalities. You should be the kind of person who lights up a Slack channel and brings energy to every call.

- Hard-working and fast. You move quickly. You don't sit on tasks. When you finish something, you go looking for the next thing rather than waiting to be told.

- Incredible attention to detail. You notice the typo, the missing field, the date that doesn't quite line up. Details are not negotiable in this role.

- Excellent written and spoken communication. You write clean, friendly, professional English using Australian English spelling. You can pick up a phone and have a quick warm conversation with a client without needing a script.

- Proactive and takes initiative, safely. You don't wait to be told. You spot things, raise them, and propose solutions. You also know where the line is: you check in before doing anything that affects clients commercially or strategically.

- AI-curious and AI-capable. You already use AI tools daily (Claude, ChatGPT, or similar). You're excited to learn how to use them better and to build on the AI tooling we're rolling out internally.

- Technologically capable. You can pick up new SaaS tools quickly. You're comfortable in CRMs, project management tools, ticketing systems, and you'd happily poke around in a HubSpot workflow editor or a Teamwork report to figure out how something works.

- Time zone overlap. Our working hours are 9am to 5:30p
lbourne time and require your work hours to fit within these constraints.



Nice to Have

- Previous experience in an agency, consulting studio, or customer success environment.

- Any familiarity with HubSpot, Teamwork, Fireflies, Slack, Notion, Claude or Gmail.

- Previous usage of the CRM HubSpot is highly desirable, but we will teach you regardless.

- Experience supporting a remote team across time zones.

- A genuine interest in CRM, marketing operations, or revenue operations.



Why This Role Matters

- You'll free up our Senior Account Manager to focus on client strategy and relationships, not admin.

- You'll free up our Senior Solutions Architect to focus on client delivery, not admin.

- You'll be the reason that clients never feel forgotten, even on quiet weeks.

- You'll be the reason that nothing important slips through the cracks.

- You'll have a direct, measurable impact on King Henry's quality of service and profitability.



Why Join King Henry?

- Work with a small, supportive, ambitious team that values your growth.

- Fully remote with flexibility around how and when you work.

- Be part of one of the fastest-growing HubSpot Solutions Partners in Australia.

- Work in a business that takes AI seriously. You'll be using and helping shape internal AI tools every day.

- Real career progression. As we grow, this role grows with you. We're explicitly hiring this as a foundational position, not a dead-end admin role.



Where Can You Grow?

- Build out the Account Management playbook alongside the Senior Account Manager.

- Take on more direct client relationship work as you build trust and HubSpot knowledge.

- Move into a full Account Manager role over time, with your own portfolio of clients.

- Help shape and run the onboarding Program for future Junior Account Managers as we scale.

- Become a HubSpot certified professional, fully funded by King Henry.

title: Collections Specialist
description:
Join Binners - a fast-growing construction services company in Toronto, Canada.



Binners provides bin rentals, demolition, waste removal, and construction support services across the Greater Toronto Area. Reliability is our brand — and we’re looking for someone who embodies that same standard in managing our receivables and collections.



If you’re organized, assertive, and thrive in a fast-paced environment, we want you on our team.



-- About the Role 

As our Collections Specialist, you will play a crucial role in managing our accounts receivable and keeping cash flow healthy. You’ll communicate daily with contractors, clients, and internal teams to ensure invoices are accurate, payments are collected on time, and any issues are resolved quickly and professionally.



This is a full-time remote role (PH-based), working Toronto business hours.



-- Responsibilities

Your day-to-day will include:

- Following up on outstanding invoices via phone, text, and

- Maintaining accurate records of collections activities 

- Sending statements, reminders, and follow-up notices

- Coordinating with our operations and accounting teams to resolve disputes

- Monitoring payment commitments and ensuring timely follow-through

- Updating client information and payment statuses in our CRM

- Reporting weekly on collections progress, outstanding balances, and actionable items

- Helping improve and refine our invoicing and collections processes



-- Requirements

- Experience in collections or accounts receivable (construction industry experience is a big plus)

- Strong communication skills - clear, confident, and professional

- Comfortable calling clients directly and handling difficult conversations

- Highly organized, with strong attention to detail

- Ability to work independently and hit deadlines

- Familiarity with QuickBooks Online, CRMs, or similar systems

- Stable internet connection, reliable computer, and quiet workspace

- Willing to work Toronto timezone (EST) hours



-- Nice to Have

- Experience working with Canadian or U.S. companies

- Background in construction, logistics, or field services

- Understanding of NET terms, COD accounts, and collections processes

- Ability to analyze aging reports and recommend action steps

title: Short Form Video Editor & Animator
description:
We’re looking for a talented Short Form Video Editor & Animator to create punchy, high-performing 30 second videos for social media. This role is all about speed, clarity, and engaging visuals that stop the scroll.



What you’ll do

• Edit short form videos (up to 30 seconds) for platforms like Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, and YouTube Shorts

• Add motion graphics, text animations, captions, and light effects to enhance engagement

• Turn raw footage, screen recordings, or existing assets into polished social content

• Adapt videos to different aspect ratios and platform best practices

• Follow brand guidelines while bringing creative ideas to the table

• Iterate quickly based on feedback and performance insights



What we’re looking for

• Strong experience editing short form social videos

• Solid motion graphics and animation skills

• Proficiency in tools like Premiere Pro, After Effects, CapCut, or similar

• A good eye for pacing, typography, and visual hierarchy

• Understanding of social media trends and what performs well

• Ability to work fast without sacrificing quality

• Reliable, communicative, and comfortable working async



Nice to have

• Experience editing content for B2B, SaaS brands or for Events

• Basic sound design or music syncing skills

• Familiarity with subtitle styling and accessibility best practices



What success looks like

• Videos are clear, engaging, and on brand

• Content feels native to each platform, not recycled

• Fast turnaround times and minimal revisions

• Social content that actually gets watched, not skipped

Apply here:

title: Packaging Graphic Designer
description:
Type: Full-time | 8 am to 5 pm Pacific

Location: Remote

Rate: $8/hr - $8.50/hr

 

Role Overview



We're looking for a skilled Packaging Graphic Designer to own the visual execution of packaging and labeling across our hard goods product lines. You'll work closely with product, sourcing, and sales teams to develop packaging that communicates clearly on shelf, meets retail compliance standards, and elevates brand perception. This role spans the full lifecycle — from concept and dieline setup through vendor-ready print files.



Key Responsibilities

-Design retail-ready packaging for hard goods including gift, home décor, kitchen, and seasonal categories — boxes, clamshells, insert cards, hang tags, labels, and sleeves

-Build and manage dielines from vendor specifications; coordinate with overseas factories and domestic print vendors on file prep and proofing

-Develop copy layout and typography hierarchies in partnership with product and marketing teams

-Ensure all packaging meets retailer-specific requirements (Walmart, Target, TJX, etc.) including barcode placement, compliance icons, and copy standards

-Maintain organized, version-controlled file libraries across active SKUs; manage revision rounds efficiently

-Support photo-real mock-up needs for sales presentations, line sheets, and trade show materials

-Collaborate with sourcing and compliance to keep material callouts, country-of-origin statements, and regulatory marks accurate and current

-Assist with product photography art direction when needed



Qualifications

-3–6 years of packaging design experience in a consumer goods, wholesale, or private label environment

-Strong command of Adobe Illustrator, Photoshop, and InDesign; experience with structural dielines is essential

-Demonstrated hard goods packaging portfolio (not just brand or digital work)

-Experience preparing production-ready files for overseas manufacturers and/or domestic printers

-Working knowledge of print specs: bleed, trim, CMYK, spot color, and finish callouts

-Familiarity with major retailer packaging compliance requirements

-Experience with import/wholesale consumer product companies

-Knowledge of Acrobat preflight and print-ready PDF workflows

-Exposure to ERP preferably NetSuite systems for asset management

-Experience supporting new product development timelines with tight deadlines



To apply: Please submit your application through the link below:

title: Operations Manager VA (with US CPG Experience)

title: Experienced Cold Caller / Telemarketer for U.S. Insurance Leads
description:
Job Title: Experienced Cold Caller / Telemarketer for U.S. Insurance Leads

Company: DialXpress

Location: Remote

Job Type: Full-time

Shift: U.S. business hours, Monday to Friday

Experience Required: Minimum 2 years cold calling or telemarketing experience



About Us

DialXpress is a U.S.-based outbound calling company serving insurance agencies nationwide. The company focuses on calling x-dates, internet leads, aged leads, former customers, and requotes, with a live-caller model only. DialXpress positions itself as an approved vendor and emphasizes compliant calling, high dial volume, and warm handoffs for quoting.



About the Role

We are hiring an experienced cold caller / telemarketer who knows how to get through lists, handle objections, speak clearly, and generate real conversations with U.S. prospects. This is not an entry-level role. We want someone who is comfortable making high-volume outbound calls, following call flows, updating the CRM properly, and helping produce quote opportunities for insurance agencies.



You will be calling leads provided by the client or campaign, using approved scripts and processes. DialXpress provides the staffing side of the operation, while clients provide the lead data, dialer access, and campaign instructions.



What You’ll Be Doing



Make outbound calls to U.S. leads daily

Work internet leads, aged leads, x-dates, former customers, and requote opportunities

Speak with prospects professionally and confidently

Follow campaign scripts while still sounding natural

Qualify interest and transfer or hand off warm prospects when appropriate

Log call outcomes, notes, dispositions, and follow-up actions correctly

Follow compliance rules and do-not-call procedures

Maintain strong daily activity and productivity standards

Communicate with management about lead quality, objections, and call results



What We’re Looking For



At least 2 years of experience in cold calling, telemarketing, appointment setting, or outbound sales

Strong spoken English with a clear and neutral accent

Confident on live calls

Good objection handling skills

Comfortable with repetitive outbound work and performance tracking

Reliable internet and quiet work environment

Experience calling U.S. consumers or business owners is preferred

Insurance lead calling experience is a major plus

Experience using dialers, CRMs, and call disposition systems is preferred



Important Requirements



Must be comfortable making a high volume of outbound calls daily (400+)

Must follow scripts, compliance rules, and company processes

Must update records accurately after calls

Must show up consistently and be dependable

Must be coachable and open to feedback



What Makes This Role Different



Live-caller environment only

No robocalls

No AI voice

Warm handoff style campaigns when applicable

DialXpress is built around lead engagement and structured follow-up cadence, including immediate engagement and extended follow-up windows.



Compensation



Hourly rate: $2.00-$3.00

Full-time schedule: 9am-8pm EST (9am-5pm EST Early Shift) (12pm-8pm Late Shift)

Start date: ASAP



How to Apply

To apply, send:



Your resume

A short voice recording introducing yourself

A summary of your cold calling / telemarketing experience

Any experience you have with insurance, live transfers, appointment setting, or U.S. outbound calling

Your internet speed and computer setup



Application Screening Question

Please answer these with your application:



How many years of cold calling experience do you have?

Have you called U.S. leads before?

What industries have you called in?

Are you comfortable with high-volume outbound calling every day?

Have you worked with dialers and CRMs before?

What is your expected hourly rate?



If you want, I can turn this into a more aggressive OnlineJobs.ph-style version optimized to attract stronger sales reps instead of general telemarketers.

title: Outbound Sales Closer | $3,000-$6,000/mo Realistic | US SaaS Startup | Hiring Now
description:
HIRING THIS WEEK: Outbound Closer for US SaaS Startup. Commission-Heavy. No Cap.



I'm Maya. I run Vault. We help contractors stop losing money from missed phone calls.



When a contractor is on a job site and misses a call, Vault texts the customer back in 15 seconds. Simple product. Obvious value. The data sells itself.



I need one person who can get on the phone, offer something free, and close when the numbers do the talking.





THE JOB



You make two types of calls.



Call 1 is the easiest call you'll ever make. You're offering contractors a free 7-day audit that tracks every call they miss and calculates how much money they're losing. Free. No commitment. Takes them 30 seconds to set up. You're not selling anything on this call. You're giving them something valuable for free.



Call 2 is where you get paid. After 7 days the audit spits out a report. "You missed 26 calls last week. That's $94,000 a year walking out the door." You call them, walk them through their numbers, and ask if they want to keep it running for $149/month. The data is so brutal that most contractors say yes on the spot. You're not convincing anyone. You're just showing them their own money disappearing.





WHAT'S ALREADY DONE FOR YOU



You are not sourcing leads. You are not building lists. You are not cold
ing. You are not doing any admin work.



Every morning you get a fresh call list with the contractor's name, phone number, city, Google rating, and a note telling you exactly what to say. Power dialer is loaded with a US local number. Voi
 drop is recorded. SMS templates are ready to send. You open your laptop, put on your headset, and dial. That's your entire job.





PAY



$2/hr base. $75 per customer who pays. No cap. Ever.



Here's what that actually looks like:



2 closes per day: $166/day, $3,320/month

3 closes per day: $241/day, $4,820/month

4+ closes per day: $316+/day, $6,320+/month



Weekly pay through Wise or PayPal. I have never paid late and I never will.



Hit 15 paying customers in your first month and two things happen: your base goes to $3/hr permanently, and you become the sales lead. When I hire the next caller, you train them and earn overrides on their closes. This is a ground floor role at a company that is scaling fast.





WHO I'M LOOKING FOR



You've done outbound before. BPO, call center, remote sales, freelance, whatever. You've made 200+ dials in a day and it didn't faze you. You hear "commission-heavy" and you get excited because you trust your own ability. You don't need someone checking on you every hour. You show up, you dial, you perform.



Your English must be clear with a neutral or American-passing accent. You are calling US contractors. If they can't understand you or if you sound like you're in a call center overseas, they hang up. This is non-negotiable.





DO NOT APPLY IF



You've never done outbound calling.

You need a high base salary to feel safe.

200 dials/day sounds like a lot to you.

You're going to ask me for training on how to make phone calls.





SCHEDULE



8 hours/day, Monday through Friday. 8am to 5pm US Mountain Time with a 1 hour break. 200+ dials per day minimum.





HOW TO APPLY



Do all 3 or I won't read your application.



1. Record yourself saying this out loud. Not reading it. Say it like you're actually on the phone with a contractor named Mike and you're delivering his report:



"Hey Mike, your Vault report just came in. Over the last 7 days you missed 26 calls. Based on your average job value, that's about $94,000 a year in lost revenue. The good news is Vault caught every one of those and texted your customers back within 8 seconds. Want to keep it running? It's $149 a month."



Upload or link the recording. This is how I evaluate your accent and delivery. If you sound natural and confident, you're probably getting hired. If you sound like you're reading, you're not.



2. Tell me about your outbound experience. Where you worked, how many dials per day, what you were selling, and roughly what your close rate was.



3. Tell me when you can start.



I'm hiring this week. First qualified person who applies and sounds great on the recording gets the trial.

title: Social Media Manager
description:
Here's a clean job post ready for OnlineJobs.ph:



TikTok Clipping VA — Skiing & Snowboarding App (Full-Time)

We are hiring a full-time TikTok Clipping VA for Shredder, a skiing and snowboarding social app based in the US. You will run three TikTok pages, edit short-form clips daily, and help grow our brand to a massive audience.



What You'll Do

You will manage three TikTok clipping pages dedicated to skiing and snowboarding content. Each month, you are responsible for posting 300 videos across those pages, generating at least 1,000,000 combined views, and maintaining a consistent standard of quality across every post. This is a full-time role — expect to produce roughly 10 videos per day.

You will source clips from videos we provide, edit them into engaging short-form content, add our branding, and post daily on TikTok. Instagram and YouTube are handled automatically through a tool we set up together — your only manual posting platform is TikTok.



Requirements



Proven experience editing short-form video content (TikTok, Reels, or Shorts)

Strong eye for what makes a clip engaging — pacing, music, transitions, and hook

Ability to produce high output without sacrificing quality

Reliable internet connection and the ability to work full-time hours

Familiarity with TikTok and how the algorithm rewards consistency

Attention to detail — branding, formatting, and posting standards must be followed exactly





Nice to Have



Interest in skiing, snowboarding, or action sports

Experience with CapCut, Adobe Premiere, or similar editing tools

Prior experience running multiple social media pages





What We Offer



$1,000 USD per month, paid at the end of each 30-day period

Full-time, fully remote position

Clear expectations — we provide the content, you handle the editing and posting

A fast-growing brand with real traction (100k+ downloads, 60M+ organic views)





Payment Terms

Payment is issued in full each month when all three requirements are met: 300 videos posted, 1,000,000+ combined views, and consistent video quality. We track performance transparently and will communicate with you throughout the month.

title: Full-Time Outbound Caller + Closer for US SaaS Company (Commission-Heavy, High Earners Only)
description:
We're Vault, a US SaaS company. We help contractors recover lost revenue from missed calls. We need one experienced, full-time outbound caller + closer. You must have outbound calling experience . You already know how to dial, pitch, handle objections, and close.



WHAT'S ALREADY BUILT FOR YOU:

- Power dialer loaded and ready (you press call, it dials)

- CRM with full pipeline management

- Lead lists loaded weekly (you never source leads)

- Automated audit system that runs 7 days and generates reports

- US phone number provided

- Word-for-word scripts (you can adjust based on your style)



YOUR 3 JOBS:



1. OUTBOUND — 200+ dials per day minimum:

Call contractors from the power dialer. Offer a free 7-day trial that monitors their missed calls and shows them how much revenue they're losing. You walk them through a 30-second setup on their phone. You're offering something free. Easy yes.



2. FOLLOW UP — report delivery calls:

After 7 days, a report auto-generates showing how many calls the contractor missed and how much money they lost. You call them. Walk them through the numbers. The data is brutal — contractors see $20,000-$100,000/year walking out the door. The report does the convincing.



3. CLOSE — ask for the sale:

After delivering the report you ask: "Want to keep this running? $147 a month." Most say yes because the math is obvious — $147/month to save $50,000+/year. You handle any remaining objections and get the card.



YOU SHOULD APPLY IF:

- You've worked outbound in a BPO, call center, or remote sales role

- You've made 200+ dials/day before and it doesn't scare you

- You know how to handle objections without freezing

- You've closed deals over the phone before (even small ones)

- You don't need someone holding your hand

- You hear "commission-heavy" and get excited, not nervous



DO NOT APPLY IF:

- You've never done outbound calling

- You need extensive training on how to make calls

- You're looking for a high base salary with no performance pressure

- 200 dials/day sounds like a lot to you



PAY STRUCTURE (commission-heavy — designed for closers):



Base: $1.50/hour ($12/day, $250/month floor)



Commissions:

- $10 per free trial you set up and get running

- $75 per trial that converts to a paying customer ($147/month)

- $150 per customer that upgrades to $499/month (happens after a few months, easy upsell)



NO CAP. EVER.



DAILY EARNINGS AT 200+ DIALS:



Solid performer — 6 trials/day, 2 closes/day:

$12 base + $60 trials + $150 closes = $222/day = $4,440/month



Strong performer — 8 trials/day, 3 closes/day:

$12 + $80 + $225 = $317/day = $6,340/month



Animal — 10 trials/day, 4 closes/day:

$12 + $100 + $300 = $412/day = $8,240/month



Weekly pay via Wise or PayPal. Never late.



THE PRODUCT SELLS ITSELF:

The first call is offering something free. The second call is delivering data that shocks them — "you missed 28 calls worth $94,000 last year." You're not convincing anyone of anything. You're showing them their own numbers and asking if they want to keep making money. This is the easiest close you'll ever make.



SCHEDULE:

8 hours/day, Monday-Friday

US business hours (8am-5pm Central, 1-hour break)

200+ dials per day is the minimum expectation. Non-negotiable.



REQUIREMENTS:

- Minimum 6 months outbound calling experience (BPO, call center, remote sales, freelance — any counts)

- Clear English with neutral or American-passing accent

- Quiet workspace, fast internet, quality headset

- Comfortable with power dialers and CRMs

- 200+ dials/day is your normal, not your stretch goal

- Reliable. On time. Every single day.

- Hungry. You see uncapped commissions and you want to destroy your targets.



THIS IS A GROUND-FLOOR ROLE. We are scaling fast. First person in the door who performs becomes the sales lead and builds the team. That means higher base, override commissions on your team's closes, and leadership title. But you earn it with results first.



HOW TO APPLY — ALL 4 REQUIRED:



1. Record yourself reading this script naturally (not robotic, not reading — deliver it like you're actually on the phone with a contractor):



"Hey Mike, your Vault report just came in. Over the last 7 days you missed 26 calls. Based on your average job value, that's about $94,000 a year in lost revenue. The good news — Vault caught every one of those and texted your customers back within 8 seconds. Want to keep it running? It's $147 a month."



2. Attach the recording



3. Tell me your outbound calling experience — where you worked, how many dials/day, what you sold, what your close rate was



4. Tell me when you can start



No recording = automatic rejection. I'm hiring this week.

There is also clear potential for advancement into acquisitions or leadership roles for top performers.



We are a fast-paced, results-driven team focused on growth and continuous improvement, and we are looking for someone who wants to grow with us long term.

title: LinkedIn Outreach Assistant — 24-Day Project, ~5 Min/Day
description:
WHAT'S THE WORK?



We're hiring 40+ part-time assistants for a 24-day LinkedIn outreach campaign starting late April 2026. The work is 100% copy-paste: open a candidate's LinkedIn profile, paste a pre-written InMail message, send, mark complete in a tracking sheet.



- ~5 minutes per day

- ~3.5 hours total over 24 days

- 100% scripted — no message writing, no replies, no decisions

- Pre-prepared queue delivered to you daily

- You will NOT respond to candidate replies — you forward them to our internal team

- Skip days are fine. Weekends, family stuff, sick days — your queue rolls forward, just keep up roughly with the daily pace overall





WHAT YOU EARN



$0.35 per message verified. About 75 messages over the campaign = $26.25 total.



Choose how you get paid:



- WEEKLY PAYMENTS via USDT (Solana network), bank transfer, or mobile top-up — paid every Friday

- OR SINGLE PAYMENT at the end of the campaign + $5 completion bonus = $31.25 total



Top performers also get first invitation to Phase 2 and Phase 3 of this campaign over the following 60 days (additional ~$30-50 each, not guaranteed).





WHAT YOU NEED



1. A LINKEDIN PROFILE IN ONE OF THESE FIELDS: recruiting, talent acquisition, HR, executive search, executive/personal assistant, or virtual assistant. Profiles in unrelated fields (graphic design, marketing, sales/SDR, lead generation, software development, healthcare, customer service, etc.) will not be considered — even if your past work history includes some recruiting experience. Your current LinkedIn headline matters most.



2. A LinkedIn account at least 1 year old, with a professional photo and (ideally) 50+ connections.



3. YOU HAVE NOT USED A LINKEDIN SALES NAVIGATOR FREE TRIAL IN THE PAST 12 MONTHS (we'll verify with a 2-minute screenshot check before hiring).



4. ~5 minutes most days for ~24 days.





ABOUT US



We're Obsidian Global, a business group running this campaign for three companies. Our coordinator Piumantha is your main point of contact.



We pay every freelancer who completes the work, on time, in full, via your chosen method. Weekly payments option exists specifically for freelancers who prefer regular payouts.





HOW TO APPLY



Reply with:



1. A SCREENSHOT OF YOUR LINKEDIN PROFILE (showing photo, headline, current role, connection count)



2. Confirmation: "I have NOT used a Sales Navigator trial in the past 12 months"



3. ONE SENTENCE ANSWERING THIS: What is our policy on responding to candidate replies?



Piumantha responds to qualified applicants within 24 hours during weekdays.

title: Customer Success Manager (HubSpot Specialist)
description:
We are a specialized delivery consultancy implementing HubSpot for high-growth businesses. We’re looking for a proactive, relationship-driven Customer Success Manager (CSM) who lives and breathes client ROI and platform adoption.



In this role, you aren’t just "managing accounts"—you are a strategic partner. You will bridge the gap between technical configuration and business outcomes, ensuring our clients don’t just use HubSpot, but thrive because of it.



What You’ll Be Doing



Strategic Advisory: Partnering with high-growth clients to align their business goals with HubSpot’s capabilities, moving them from initial implementation to long-term maturity.



Adoption & Retention: Monitoring platform health and usage metrics to proactively identify risks and opportunities for deeper tool integration.



Solution Mapping: Translating business pain points into strategic roadmaps, collaborating with our technical team to ensure configurations deliver real-world value.



Client Enablement: Leading workshops and training sessions to ensure client teams are confident, competent, and getting the most out of their HubSpot investment.



Account Growth: Identifying opportunities for upsells, cross-fills, and renewals by consistently demonstrating the "why" behind the "how."



What We’re Looking For



Experience: 3+ years in Customer Success, Account Management, or Consulting, specifically within the SaaS or CRM ecosystem.



HubSpot Literacy: A strong understanding of the HubSpot suite (Marketing, Sales, Service, and Operations Hubs). You don't need to be a developer, but you must know what the platform can do.



The "Guide" Mindset: An empathetic communicator who can simplify complex technical concepts and lead clients through change management.



Data-Driven: Comfortable analyzing KPIs and reporting on ROI to prove the value of our consultancy's work.



Commitment: This is a full-time employee position. We are looking for a dedicated partner to become the face of our client relationships.



The Opportunity



Competitive Salary: A stable, full-time base salary that reflects your relationship management expertise.



Payment Terms: Remuneration is paid in AUD only.



High-Impact Work: Manage a portfolio of diverse, high-growth companies where your guidance directly influences their scaling journey.



Professional Growth: Stay at the forefront of the HubSpot ecosystem with a consultancy that prioritizes the platform's latest features.



Modern Culture: Join a team that values technical excellence, precision, and collaborative problem-solving.



How to Apply



Ready to drive growth for the next generation of businesses? Please send us a message and a brief summary of a successful client outcome you’ve led.



Note: This is a permanent, full-time employee role. Please ensure you are comfortable with the payment structure in AUD before applying.

title: Senior Accountant
description:
Join our team as a Senior Accountant in the dynamic Real Estate sector. In this role, you will work closely with our Head Controller and Property Managers to oversee the finance and accounting operations company-wide. This full-time position, based on PST (Pacific Standard Time), offers substantial opportunities for career advancement based on demonstrated ability and knowledge.



- Generate monthly and quarterly financial reports as required

- Produce financial statements, conduct detailed variance analysis, and reconcile general ledger accounts for individual entities

- Perform bank reconciliations and journal entries

- Ensure accuracy in general ledger, manage accounts payable, payroll, and cash flow

- Prepare journal entries with supporting documentation across all entities

- Assist in budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis

- Maintain invoice tracking, ensuring approvals and alignment with PO systems

- Reconcile year-end payroll tax reports and oversee issuance of W2’s and 1099s

- Establish and enforce internal controls

- Contribute to enhancements of accounting systems

- Assist in creating and updating process documentation



Required Skills:



- Strong commitment to timeliness and high-quality work

- Ability to organize and prioritize tasks with minimal supervision

- Proficient in GAAP and financial reporting

- Exceptional work ethic with a goal-oriented mindset

- Advanced skills in Microsoft Office and Excel

- Experience with accounting systems; familiarity with AppFolio is advantageous

- Preference for candidates with prior real estate industry experience



Education:

- Bachelor’s degree in Accountancy or related field



About Us:



Stoneridge Management Partners, Inc. (“SMP”) is a full-service commercial property management company specializing in multi-family apartments. Our primary focus is in the Los Angeles, San Bernardino, Riverside, and Orange counties. At Stoneridge Management Partners, we make renting in Southern California easy. We aim to combine industry best practices with technology and mobile tools to modernize property management and become a leader in the industry. We encourage you to learn more about our services and to connect with us to discuss your property.



Join us and advance your career in accounting with limitless growth opportunities in our thriving Real Estate company.

title: Research & Opportunity Assistant (Part-Time)
description:
I am looking for a reliable and detail-oriented assistant with strong intuition to support deep online research and actionable data collection.



This role involves the researching and identifying opportunities such as grants, programs, internships, and relevant people. Information, and in some cases data, will need to be collected, organized, and presented clearly to support effective decision-making. Clear and accurate English writing is necessary, the ability to phone call is good, but I do not require it.



Research will primarily focus on opportunities in Canada, the United States, India, and China.



Initiative and slightly unconventional thinking are strong assets. I am looking for someone who can go beyond basic search results and identify opportunities that are not immediately obvious. Comfort using AI models is expected, but they should be used thoughtfully (helpful for initial research and exploration, but final details and verification should come from reliable sources and your own judgment).



Example task:

“Identify 4 individuals connected to high levels of government in India who have a history of developing foreign talent, and determine the most effective way to contact them.”



This is the type of thinking and output I am looking for.



This is a part-time, long-term role with the potential to increase hours and pay based on performance. The role will begin at approximately 3 hours per day, with an increase to 4 hours per day once a consistent system and workflow are established.



Shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete a paid test task.



To apply, include the word Monk at the top of your message.

title: Real Estate Photo Editor/Quality Control Specialist
description:
Are you someone who cannot let an imperfect image go, not because you were told to catch it, but because it genuinely bothers you?

We are looking for a Photo QC Editor for our growing Real Estate Media Agency based in Los Angeles, CA! Someone who takes real pride in the details others miss. Someone who looks at a gallery and knows right away when something is off, a color cast, a crooked horizon, an inconsistent edit, before anyone has to point it out.

This role is a great fit for someone who is careful, self-directed, and genuinely cares about the quality of the work they put out. You do not rush through a batch just to get it done. You understand that every delivery represents a client's first impression, and you treat it that way.

At our company, quality control is not a final checkbox. It is a standard we build into everything we do.



What You Will Do

- Photo Quality Review Review photo deliverables before they go to clients. You will check that edits are clean and consistent, flag anything that does not meet our standards, and make sure files are properly labeled and organized. This includes light Lightroom editing when needed and Photoshop object removal.

- Video Quality Review Do a basic pass on video deliverables to confirm they are complete, properly labeled, and ready for delivery.

- 3D Tour and Floor Plan Review Check Matterport 3D tours and Zillow 3D tours for accuracy and completeness. Make sure everything is properly uploaded and showing correctly on the right platforms.

- Administrative Support Help track daily project submissions, upload deliverables to the correct platforms, and handle light admin tasks that keep things running smoothly. On Saturdays, later availability is preferred to support any additional admin needs that come up.



You Will Do Well Here If:

You have a strong eye and genuinely care about quality

You can manage your own work without needing someone to follow up with you

You communicate clearly and do not wait to be chased for updates

You stay focused and organized even when the workload is heavy

You take ownership of your work from start to finish



Requirements

You must have one of the following backgrounds:

- Worked as a real estate photo editor

- Strong QC experience specifically within real estate media

Without one of these, this role will not be the right fit.



You also need:

- Hands-on experience with Adobe Photoshop and Lightroom

- Sharp attention to detail

- Clear written and verbal English communication

- Fast, stable internet connection for handling large media files



Bonus Qualifications:

- Experience with Matterport, Zillow 3D, or similar platforms

- Experience using AI editing tools (strong plus)

- Familiarity with organizing or reviewing large batches of real estate media



Schedule and Availability

- Tuesday through Saturday, Los Angeles time (PST)

- Approximately 6:00 AM to 10:00 AM PST initially (approximately 9:00 PM to 1:00 AM Philippine Standard Time) - might be later if role expands

- Around 15 to 20 hours per week to start, with potential to grow

- Saturdays: later availability preferred to support end-of-week admin needs

- Training period may require earlier hours and closer coordination



What We Offer

We are a small, close-knit team that genuinely invests in the people who work with us. This is not a role where you will feel like a number. Your work is seen, your feedback is heard, and your growth matters to us.



- Monthly tea
etings where the whole team comes together to align, share updates, and stay connected

- Regular one-on-ones with your manager so you always have direct support and honest feedback

- Year-end bonus opportunities for tea
mbers who show up consistently and contribute to growth

- Room to grow as the company scales, including the potential to lead our transition into AI-assisted editing workflows

- A supportive, high-communication culture where over-communicating is encouraged, problems are solved together, and people lead with a smile

- We take our work seriously. We do not take ourselves too seriously. If you want a stable, long-term remote opportunity where you can actually grow, this is it.



How to Apply

Please
 us at 
 share the following:

- Your experience using Photoshop, Lightroom, and editing real estate photos and media specifically

- Confirmation that you are available during the schedule described in this post

- Your working setup, including your equipment and internet connection

title: Estimator - Landscaping industry
description:
Hiring on behalf of our clients. Job Description below

*To apply - provide CV, Submit introduction video / audio to 

*Immediate availability required

*EST time-zone



About the Company

We are a fast-growing North American company transforming the way landscape contractors source plant, hardscape, and related materials. Through a technology-enabled B2B marketplace, we help contractors simplify procurement, save time, reduce stress, and access competitive pricing in an industry that has traditionally relied on manual and outdated processes.



As the company continues to expand across the United States and Canada, we are looking for driven, detail-oriented tea
mbers who can thrive in a fast-paced, high-performance environment.



About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and self-driven Estimator to join our Operations team. In this role, you will be responsible for executing a high volume of quotes across the U.S. and Canada while ensuring estimates are accurate, timely, competitive, and aligned with internal quality standards.



You will work closely with senior estimators, team leads, and operations stakeholders while taking ownership of your individual performance. This is a strong opportunity for someone who enjoys working in a deadline-driven environment, is comfortable managing multiple priorities, and wants to grow within a results-oriented company.



What You’ll Do

-Prepare and execute a high volume of quotes across the U.S. and Canada with speed and accuracy

-Deliver estimates that meet internal standards for accuracy, turnaround time, and competitive pricing

-Review quote requirements carefully and identify the best solution based on customer needs, availability, cost, and profitability

-Follow established workflows, QA processes, and internal estimating procedures

-Proactively flag gaps, inconsistencies, pricing issues, or process inefficiencies

-Communicate clearly with team leads, senior estimators, and internal stakeholders

-Escalate issues early when additional review, clarification, or support is needed

-Consistently meet service-level expectations, deadlines, and performance targets

-Support senior tea
mbers with additional estimating, procurement, or operational tasks as needed

-Contribute to overall team performance by maintaining a high standard of quality, consistency, and accountability



What You’ll Bring

1–3 years of experience in estimating, quoting, procurement, operations, or a related role

Internship or early-career experience will be considered for strong candidates

Familiarity with estimating tools, quoting platforms, ERP systems, or similar software

Strong attention to detail and a commitment to accuracy

Ability to manage multiple quotes, deadlines, and priorities without sacrificing quality

Clear and concise written and verbal communication skills in English

Strong judgment and the ability to know when to escalate issues

Proactive, solution-oriented mindset

High level of ownership, consistency, and reliability under pressure

Proficiency with Microsoft Office, especially Excel



Nice to Have

Experience with U.S. and Canadian pricing, procurement, or cross-border operations

Exposure to value engineering, cost optimization, or vendor comparison

Background in landscaping, horticulture, wholesale plant materials, construction materials, or related industries

Experience working in a remote, fast-paced operations environment



Ideal Candidate Profile

The ideal candidate is someone who is sharp, organized, accurate, and comfortable working quickly. They enjoy solving problems, can handle repetitive high-volume work without losing focus, and take pride in delivering clean, reliable estimates. They are not afraid to ask questions, but they also take initiative, learn quickly, and can be trusted to manage their workload independently.

title: Recruiter
description:
THE NANNY LEAGUE — REMOTE | NATIONWIDE SEARCH



Are you someone who can read people within minutes? Do you have exceptional intuition, strong judgment, and a natural ability to connect the right people at the right time? Do you thrive in fast-paced environments where no two days look the same—and take pride in being both meticulous and proactive?



The Nanny League is seeking a highly driven, polished, and intuitive Recruiter / Placement Coordinator to join our team. This is a unique opportunity to step into a role that blends recruiting, relationship management, and high-level matchmaking for some of the most discerning families across the country.



We are not simply filling roles—we are curating long-term relationships. The ideal candidate is someone who understands nuance, can “read between the lines,” and takes genuine pride in delivering an exceptional, highly personalized experience to both families and candidates.



The ideal candidate will bring:



• 2+ years of professional experience in recruiting, administration, or executive support

• Prior experience in the nanny and/or professional childcare industry (strongly preferred)

• Exceptional written and verbal communication skills

• A proactive, self-starting mindset with strong initiative

• Impeccable organization, attention to detail, and time management

• High level of integrity, accountability, and professionalism

• A positive, solutions-oriented attitude and coachable mindset

• Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-touch environment

• Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build meaningful relationships

• Comfort working fully remote and managing multiple priorities simultaneously



Technical skills & assets:



• Proficiency with Zoom, FaceTime, WhatsApp, and virtual communication platforms

• Strong working knowledge of Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive, etc.) and Microsoft Office

• Tech-savvy with the ability to quickly learn new systems and platforms

• Typing speed of at least 36 WPM

• Active and comfortable with communication across platforms (text,
, etc.)

• Familiarity with social media platforms including Instagram,
, and LinkedIn



A significant plus:



• Experience with iCIMS, Mailchimp, Rella, Anchor, Barefoot Student, and CRM/ATS systems



In this role, you will be responsible for sourcing and recruiting top-tier candidates, screening resumes and applications, conducting in-depth interviews, and thoughtfully guiding candidates through the placement process. You will utilize applicant tracking systems and internal databases to manage pipelines, while also building and maintaining meaningful, long-term relationships with candidates. You will collaborate closely with our team to deliver clear, well-documented insights and ensure a seamless experience from start to finish.



This is a fully remote position with a highly collaborative, fast-moving team. Compensation is competitive and commensurate with experience, and includes payroll, unlimited PTO, 401(k) options, and performance-based bonuses.



If you are someone who is sharp, intuitive, highly organized, and genuinely passionate about people, we would love to hear from you.



Please submit your resume and cover letter here, or
 

 with any questions.

title: Social Media & Community Engagement Specialist (Remote)
description:
Social Media & Community Engagement Specialist (Remote)



Are you a master of short-form video with a knack for jumping into local conversations? We are a multi-state company looking for a creative, data-driven Social Media & Community Engagement Specialist to manage the online presence of two distinct local brands in Chicago: an Appliance Repair company and a Home Cleaning company.



Your mission is to build hyper-local trust. You won't just be posting to a feed; you’ll be the "neighborly expert" engaging in local subreddits,
 groups, and community forums to drive real service inquiries.





Key Responsibilities



1. Content Strategy & Short-Form Video

Appliance Repair ("The Expert"): Produce "Repair vs. Replace" advice, DIY maintenance tips, and "Save your Saturday" repair reels.

Cleaning Services ("The Relief"): Create satisfying "Before/After" transformations, "Clean-With-Me" reels, and organization hacks.

Localized Outreach: Write engaging captions tailored specifically to the Chicago market.



2. Active Community Management (The "Deep-Dive")

Reddit & Quora: Actively monitor relevant subreddits (e.g., r/chicago, r/homeowners) and Quora threads. Answer user questions about appliance failures or cleaning dilemmas to position our brands as the solution.

 & Nextdoor: Join local neighborhood groups. Instead of just "pushing" ads, provide helpful comments and expert advice to community members seeking recommendations.

?Review Management: Monitor and respond to comments, DMs, and reviews on Yelp,
, and Instagram with a professional, helpful tone.



3. Growth & Local Engagement

Promotions: Execute seasonal campaigns (e.g., "Holiday Oven Tune-up" or "Post-Winter Deep Clean").

Influencer Outreach: Connect with local Chicago micro-influencers or complementary businesses like real estate agents.

Consistency: Maintain a steady rhythm of 3–5 high-quality posts per week per brand.



4. Performance Tracking

Report monthly on Reach, Engagement, and Inbound Leads.

Track the success of community engagement (referrals from Reddit/Facebook groups).





Requirements & Qualifications



Experience: 1–3 years in Social Media Management (Local Service/Home Service experience is a major plus).



Video Mastery: High proficiency in CapCut and Canva. You should be able to turn raw footage into trending, high-energy reels.



Community Native: You understand the "vibe" of 
 and
 groups—knowing how to be helpful without sounding like a spammer.



Tone: Excellent written English with a "neighborly" yet professional voice.

Tools: Experience with Meta Business Suite, Buffer, or similar scheduling tools.

Btw include the word "supreme" in the subject line.





Why Join Us?



Flexibility: Work from anywhere with flexible hours.



Brand Building: The opportunity to shape two distinct brand identities from the ground up within a growing company.



Compensation: $500 per month.





How to Apply



Please send your portfolio or links to accounts you currently manage.

Bonus Points: Include a 30-second video introducing yourself!

title: Real Estate Outreach VA
description:
I’m looking for a VA who can help contact property owners and decision-makers for off-market real estate opportunities.



This is not basic data entry.



You need to be comfortable calling people, asking simple questions, taking notes, and helping figure out who the right person is to speak with.



You will be given specific properties to work on. Your job is to help find the owner, relative, manager, tenant, neighbor, or decision-maker connected to the property and see if there may be interest in a real estate conversation. Base pay plus commission.



You do not need to close deals.



You do need to be sharp, consistent, honest, and willing to keep trying when the first number does not work.



Tasks may include:



Calling property owners and related contacts

Sending simple texts when needed

Looking up relatives or connected people

Taking clean notes

Updating a spreadsheet

Reporting back clearly

Handing off warm conversations to me



This role is best for someone who is confident speaking with people.



I care more about effort, communication, and common sense than years of experience.



To apply, answer these questions:



Have you done cold calling or real estate outreach before?

Are you comfortable calling people in the United States?

What hours can you work in Eastern Time?

Send a short voice recording introducing yourself and explaining why you would be good for this role.

If the first phone number does not answer, what would you do next?



When you apply as the subject in the
 the state you would visit first if you were to visit the US one day. This shows me that you have read the instructions and shows me you're real. Make sure only the state appears in the subject line. 



Applications without the subject line and voice recording will not be reviewed.

title: Supplier Development Coordinator
description:
Hiring on behalf of our clients. Job Description below

*To apply - provide CV, Submit introduction video / audio to 

*Immediate availability required

*EST time-zone

*English FLUENCY is mandatory



About the Role

We are looking for a proactive and organized Supplier Development Coordinator to help grow and manage a supplier network across North America.



In this role, you will be on the frontline of supplier acquisition and relationship building. You will be responsible for identifying new suppliers, conducting outbound outreach, qualifying supplier fit, collecting inventory lists and catalogs, and supporting a smooth onboarding process.



This role sits at the intersection of sales outreach, procurement support, data management, and cross-team coordination. You will work closely with Inventory Management and Operations teams to ensure supplier information is accurate, complete, and properly handed off.



The ideal candidate is a natural communicator who is comfortable picking up the phone, working through outreach lists, staying organized across multiple conversations, and taking initiative without needing constant direction.



What You’ll Do

-Supplier Acquisition & Qualification

-Conduct high-volume outbound outreach by phone and
 to prospective suppliers

-Research and identify new supplier prospects across target regions

-Acquire supplier inventory lists, product catalogs, availability sheets, and business information

-Qualify new supplier leads based on product fit, geography, service reliability, and internal supplier standards

-Manage the supplier onboarding process from first contact through qualification, documentation, and internal handoff

-Ask clear discovery questions to understand supplier capabilities, coverage areas, inventory, pricing process, and responsiveness



Supplier Relationship Management

-Maintain ongoing supplier relationships through regular follow-up and communication

-Follow up on open items, missing information, inventory updates, and onboarding requirements

-Help resolve friction points during supplier onboarding or ongoing communication

-Support partnership alignment by keeping suppliers informed, responsive, and engaged

-Process and organize incoming supplier catalogs and inventory updates according to internal SOPs

-Manage and triage supplier communications through the inventory inbox



Data & CRM Management

-Keep CRM records accurate and up to date with supplier contacts, company information, notes, status, and next steps

-Accurately complete and process supplier/customer request forms

-Coordinate with the Inventory Management team to hand off updated availability and flag data issues

-Maintain strong data hygiene so internal teams can rely on supplier records and inventory information

-Process & Knowledge Building

-Support process documentation and contribute to internal knowledge-building around the supplier network

-Identify recurring issues, gaps, or inefficiencies in supplier outreach, onboarding, or data collection

-Help build a more organized, scalable supplier development process over time



What You’ll Bring

1–2 years of experience in sales, procurement, account management, business development, customer support, operations, or a coordinator-type role

Comfortable making a high volume of outbound calls and
s

Strong verbal and written English communication skills

Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple supplier conversations at once

Detail-oriented approach to data entry, CRM updates, and documentation

Self-starter mentality with the ability to work through assigned lists without constant direction

Professional, confident phone presence with no hesitation making outbound calls

Ability to follow SOPs while also using common sense and judgment

Comfort working in a fast-paced, remote team environment



Nice to Have

Familiarity with CRM tools, especially HubSpot

Experience working with suppliers, vendors, procurement teams, or wholesale/distribution businesses

Experience collecting catalogs, inventory lists, pricing sheets, or availability information

Exposure to landscaping, horticulture, construction materials, or related industries



Ideal Candidate Profile

The ideal candidate is organized, confident, and comfortable communicating with suppliers every day. They are not afraid to pick up the phone, ask direct questions, follow up consistently, and keep detailed records of every interaction.



They should be able to manage many supplier conversations at the same time without losing track of next steps. They should also understand that CRM hygiene and data accuracy are critical because the information they collect will be used by other internal teams.



This person does not need to be a senior salesperson, but they do need to be proactive, persistent, reliable, and comfortable with outbound communication.

title: Outbound Call Center Team Manager – Debt Relief (US Shift)
description:
Outbound Call Center Team Manager – Debt Relief (US Shift)

COMPANY: Lumis Financial

LOCATION: Remote – Philippines

SHIFT: US Business Hours (EST) 7am to 6pm Monday to Friday

Saturday 8am to 4pm

COMPENSATION: $1100–$1,200/month + performance commission per qualified or enrolled deal by his team. 

Must have 5 years in debt settlement industry experience.



ABOUT LUMIS FINANCIAL



Lumis Financial is a fast-growing US-based debt relief company helping American consumers reduce and settle their personal loan and credit card debt. We are expanding our Philippines operations and are looking for an exceptional Team Manager to lead, coach, and drive performance across our outbound opener team.



This is a high-responsibility leadership role. You will be the direct link between our US management team and our Philippines openers. Your team's performance directly impacts company revenue — and you will be rewarded accordingly.



THE ROLE



You will manage a team of 10–15 outbound openers responsible for calling US consumers, qualifying them for debt relief programs, and transferring live calls to our US-based closers. Your job is to ensure every opener follows the script precisely, hits their daily KPIs, and delivers high-quality transfers that convert.



You report directly to Lumis Financial's owners and senior management in the United States.



RESPONSIBILITIES



• Lead, coach, and motivate a team of 10–15 outbound openers on a daily basis

• Monitor live calls and recordings to ensure strict script adherence and call quality

• Conduct daily team huddle (10–15 minutes) to align the team on goals and performance

• Deliver daily KPI reports to US management covering: calls made, contact rate, transfer rate, and closer conversion rate

• Identify underperforming agents, provide real-time coaching, and implement improvement plans

• Onboard and train new openers on script, process, objection handling, and compliance

• Enforce TCPA compliance and calling hour restrictions across the team at all times

• Coordinate daily lead distribution — ensure data is loaded into the dialer every morning before shift starts

• Collaborate with US management to continuously improve script performance and conversion rates

• Handle team scheduling, attendance tracking, and escalate HR issues as needed

• Maintain a high-energy, accountable, performance-driven team culture



REQUIREMENTS — NON-NEGOTIABLE



ACCENT: Must speak with a neutral American English accent. This is a strict requirement — your team calls US consumers and accent must be indistinguishable from a native US English speaker. Please do not apply if you have a noticeable Filipino or foreign accent.



EXPERIENCE: Minimum 5 years of experience in the US debt relief, debt settlement, or collections industry — specifically in an outbound call center environment.



LEADERSHIP: Minimum 3 years of experience managing, coaching, and holding accountable a team of outbound callers in a performance-driven environment.



DEBT INDUSTRY KNOWLEDGE: Must have deep understanding of debt settlement, personal loan relief, credit card debt programs, hardship qualification, and US consumer debt regulations (TCPA, FDCPA basics).



RESULTS: Must be able to demonstrate a track record of improving team KPIs — contact rates, transfer rates, and close rates — through coaching and accountability.



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS



• Experience with dialers such as Convoso, PhoneBurner, MOJO, or similar platforms

• Familiarity with CRM systems such as GoHighLevel, Salesforce, or HubSpot

• Experience managing remote teams across multiple time zones

• Previous experience in a BPO environment servicing US financial services clients

• Strong written English for daily reporting to US management



KPIs YOU WILL BE HELD TO



• Team contact rate: 10–15% minimum

• Qualified transfer rate: 20–30% of contacts

• Transfer acceptance by closers: 70%+

• Daily report delivered to US management by end of shift: 100%

• Script adherence score across team: 85%+



COMPENSATION



• Base: $1,200–$1,500 USD/month (based on experience)

• Performance bonus: paid monthly based on team KPI achievement

• Long-term growth opportunity as we scale the Philippines operation



HOW TO APPLY



Send the following to [your
]:



1. Your updated resume

2. A 60–90 second VOICE RECORDING of yourself introducing your experience in the US debt industry — this is mandatory and applications without a voice recording will not be reviewed

3. A brief summary of the largest team you have managed and the KPI results you achieved



We move fast. Qualified candidates will be contacted within 48 hours.



Lumis Financial | Remote – Philippines | US Shift

title: SHORT FORM VIDEO EDITOR | Flexible Work Schedule
description:
We’re hiring someone who knows how to turn long-form content into sharp, high-retention short videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.



This role is for someone who understands pacing, hooks, and visual rhythm in short-form content, and knows how to edit with intention rather than just cutting clips.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just cutting together videos for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have strong creative instincts and know how to spot 
nts that make people stop scrolling? Are you proactive, adaptable, and detail-oriented? Do you have a sharp sense of pacing, an instinct for hooks, and a clear understanding of how short-form videos need to flow to hold attention? Do you take pride in clean edits and delivering videos that feel intentional and finished? Do our core values sound like a fit for who you are?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Short-Form Editing & Story Selection

- Review long-form videos, transcripts, or raw footage to identify short-form clip opportunities

- Select 
nts that are clear, engaging, and platform-appropriate

- Edit vertical videos optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

- Create strong hooks, clean cuts, and natural pacing

- Deliver publish-ready videos that meet brand and platform standards



Captions, Visuals & Formatting

- Add on-screen captions (burned-in or SRT as required) with proper timing and readability

- Apply basic motion, zooms, punch-ins, and visual emphasis where appropriate

- Follow platform-specific formatting (aspect ratio, duration, safe margins)



Iteration & Workflow

- Implement feedback efficiently and meet agreed turnaround times

- Maintain clear file naming and folder organization

- Track edits, revisions, and delivery status as required



Requirements:

- A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION, REQUIRED (at least 150mbps)

- Experience with Final Cut Pro X (or other editing software, such as Adobe After Effects and Premiere Pro) is required

- Strong preference for MacOS users

- Experience editing short-form vertical content for social platforms, required

- Strong understanding of short-form pacing, hooks, and retention

- Experience working from transcripts or long-form content, required

- Clean file organization and understanding of post-production workflo

- Portfolio of completed short-form video projects, required

- General understanding of brand, content marketing, YouTube, etc, required

- GREAT interpersonal and communication skills, required

- Ability to self-start, work on a tight schedule, and manage multiple project deadlines, required

- Ability to follow directions and a LOVE of completing lots of projects according to consistent specifications, required

- Growth mindset, required



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.


 



STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Video Editor with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your video editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.
