title: AI Systems Engineer / Head of AI
description:
POSITION: AI Systems Engineer / Head of AI

TYPE: Full-Time, Remote



ABOUT US



We are a DTC fitness equipment brand selling directly to customers online. We are a lean, distributed team and we have been building a serious AI-driven operation across every department including customer support, operations, marketing, and finance.



We are not looking for someone to introduce us to AI. We already have live, production AI agents running every day. We need someone to take full ownership of what we have built, unify it into one coherent system, and make it significantly better.

This is a senior, strategic role. You will report directly to the founder and work alongside a small team of people who are already using AI daily in their work.



WHAT THIS ROLE IS ABOUT



Right now we have people across different departments each building and maintaining AI systems in their own lane. One person handles reporting automation. Another handles customer support automation. Another handles marketing. Another handles operations and finance. These systems are only partially talking to each other. No single person is responsible for looking across all of it and asking: is this actually the best way to do this?



That is what you are here to do.



Even if we think what we have is the best, something better probably exists. Your job is to find it and lead us toward it.



THE INFRASTRUCTURE YOU ARE WALKING INTO



Everything runs on an on-premise brain computer connected to an always-on Cloudflare Tunnel for external access. There are no cloud servers. Two cloud surfaces sit above it: Firebase Hosting for our dashboards and the Anthropic API for model calls.



We do not use one AI model. We route per job based on cost, speed, and task:



---> Claude Opus (Anthropic) for complex reasoning and primary agent work

---> Claude Sonnet (Anthropic) for CS draft generation, auto-tagging, faster workloads

---> Claude Haiku (Anthropic) for cheap classification and triage jobs

---> Gemini Flash and Pro (Google) for our fitness program generation pipeline, some report narration, and image generation

---> GPT-4o and GPT-5 (OpenAI) for a legacy CS keyword classifier kept on OpenAI for historical reasons

Model selection is intentional. You will be responsible for setting and enforcing model selection standards across all builds, and for maintaining the internal cost-routing system (max_router.py) that routes some API calls through subscription accounts to control cost.



The team accesses AI through the Claude Desktop App via remote desktop on the shared Mac, through
-hosted bots for team-facing interactions, and through Slack for some agent triggers and outputs. We previously used an OpenAI-based system (OpenClaw) built by a technical partner; some legacy CS and ops automations still live there and need to be audited and migrated.



WHAT IS ALREADY BUILT



You are not starting from zero. These are live systems running in production:

---> REPORTING AND ANALYTICS AGENT: Pulls live data from our e-commerce platform, marketing analytics tool, and customer support platform daily. Auto-populates our CEO command dashboard. Runs CS performance reports, sales and marketing reports, cash position summaries, and inbound ticket counts. Triggered via Slack or runs on a morning schedule.

---> SLACK-FACING ASSISTANT: Handles CEO task briefings, inbox scanning, team status updates, and time-off requests with auto-deny logic for policy violations.

---> CS AUTOMATION LAYER: Auto-tags incoming support tickets using a keyword classifier. Generates AI-drafted responses for CS agents. Early-stage at-risk customer flagging that escalates cancel and return signals to priority. Currently limited by API timeout issues at large ticket volumes.

---> FITNESS PROGRAM GENERATOR: Fully automated pipeline. Customer fills out a form, submission triggers an API call to Claude with a large knowledge base system prompt, the AI generates a personalized 10-week training program, and it gets
ed to the customer as a PDF on a randomized morning schedule. Cost is roughly $0.01 per program.

---> CEO DAILY DASHBOARD: Hosted on Firebase. Tabs covering today's tasks, cash position,
 metrics, team status, video pipeline, and SaaS spend. Fully AI-populated, no manual data entry.

---> MARKETING AND OPS DASHBOARD: Built using Claude Code. Includes P&L, customer insights pulling from multiple platforms, and a marketing accountability layer.

---> CASH POSITION MODULE: Python script pulling from our accounting platform and cash flow tracker, parsing Slack for payment data via AI.

---> INTERNAL COST ROUTING SYSTEM: Routes API calls between paid API and subscription accounts based on job type to manage costs.

None of these systems are connected to each other. That is the core problem you are solving.



WHAT YOU ARE HERE TO BUILD



UNIFIED AI ARCHITECTURE

Map all existing systems, identify the gaps and redundancies, and build the connective layer so everything functions as one system instead of several disconnected ones.

HOLISTIC BUSINESS AI LAYER

The founder wants to be able to ask any business question in real time and get an answer. Example: what percentage of support tickets about order status lead to a return, and if those customers were moved to priority, what is the projected cash flow impact? Nobody can build that end to end right now. You will.

FIX THE CS AT-RISK SYSTEM

Resolve API timeout issues at large ticket volumes. Train pattern recognition on historical ticket data. D
 whether to keep the legacy keyword classifier on GPT or rebuild it on Claude, and justify the decision. Connect the drafting bot to historical data so quality improves over time.

STABILIZE THE SLACK INTEGRATION

The Claude-to-Slack integration was tested but is not in production. Build it properly so tea
mbers can get AI assistance without leaving Slack.

OWN API USAGE GOVERNANCE

Tea
mbers currently hit usage limits mid-workflow with no structure managing it. Build and enforce: model selection standards, usage monitoring, key rotation, cost controls, and maintenance of the cost-routing module.

LEAD TEAM AI TRAINING

Systematically upskill every tea
mber from CS agents to marketing to finance. Run a weekly AI knowledge-share call where primary AI users each teach one new thing per week. The goal is to make every person on the team significantly more effective so we never need to grow headcount just to get more done.

AUDIT AND MIGRATE LEGACY SYSTEMS

Full audit of remaining OpenClaw dependencies. D
 what gets rebuilt on Claude, what stays on OpenAI, what gets retired. Document and execute the migration plan.





REQUIRED SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE



TECHNICAL:

---> Deep proficiency in Claude specifically: Claude Projects, Claude API, Claude Code, system prompt architecture, tool use and MCP integration, prompt engineering for structured output and reasoning, context window management at scale, multi-agent orchestration

---> Strong judgment on model selection: knowing when to use Opus vs Sonnet vs Haiku vs Gemini vs GPT, and how to make cost vs quality tradeoffs at production scale

---> Python and scripting: the entire agent stack is Python-based with Google Apps Script integrations; you must be able to read, debug, and extend it

---> REST API integration: connecting external platforms without a full engineering team; our support, e-commerce, analytics, and accounting platforms all need to stay wired into the AI layer

---> Agentic AI architecture: multi-agent setups, memory management, tool chaining, session management, cost routing; you need to build agents that are reliable in production, not just in demos

---> Firebase and cloud hosting: dashboards run on Firebase; you need to understand deployment and the Cloudflare Tunnel setup on the Mac Mini

--->
 bot infrastructure: team-facing bots run through
; you need to be able to build on that layer

---> OpenAI and legacy system familiarity: some systems still run on OpenAI; you need to audit and migrate intelligently



LEADERSHIP:

---> Can look at multiple disconnected department-level AI systems and see the unified architecture underneath them

---> Communicates clearly with non-technical teammates across CS, ops, marketing, and finance

---> Pushes back when the current approach is not the best approach; we want and expect this

---> Teaches and elevates the team, not just builds systems for them

---> Works independently across a remote, distributed team without needing close supervision

---> Owns the weekly AI knowledge-share call and is accountable for the team's AI progress over time



FULL TOOLS AND PLATFORMS LIST

AI and Model Layer:

---> Claude (claude.ai, Claude API, Claude Code, Claude Desktop App)

---> OpenAI (GPT-4o, GPT-5)

---> Google Gemini (Flash and Pro)

---> OpenClaw (legacy OpenAI-based agent system)

Infrastructure and DevOps:

---> Mac Mini (on-premise compute)

---> Cloudflare Tunnel

---> Firebase Hosting

--->
 (bot interface layer)

---> Python

---> Google Apps Script

---> SSH (remote access)

---> session-commander and max_router.py (internal cost routing tooling)

Business Platforms (all require or have API integrations):

---> Shopify (e-commerce and orders)

---> Richpanel (CS ticketing and automation)

---> Triple Whale (marketing analytics)

---> ShipHero (fulfillment)

---> Flexport (freight and logistics)

---> Xero (accounting)

---> Gusto and Paychex (payroll)

---> Klaviyo 
 marketing)

---> Postscript (SMS marketing)

---> KnoCommerce (post-purchase surveys)

---> JudgeMe (product reviews)

Productivity and Collaboration:

---> Slack (team comms, agent triggers, bot outputs)

---> Google Workspace (Sheets, Forms, Drive, Gmail)

---> Asana (project and task management)

---> Claude.ai Projects (knowledge base and system prompt hosting)

Dashboards and Reporting Surfaces:

---> CEO Daily Dashboard (Google Sheets and Firebase)

---> Marketing Dashboard (Google Sheets and Firebase)

---> CS Dashboard (Firebase)

---> Cashflow API (Firebase)



WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

By 30 days: Full audit of all existing systems completed. Architecture map delivered. Top priorities identified and approved. Cost routing fully understood and documented.

By 60 days: CS at-risk flagging live and reliable. Slack integration stable in production. Cost governance enforced. Weekly AI call running. Team upskilling underway.

By 90 days: The unified AI layer is live. The founder can query the business holistically. All department systems are connected. Legacy system audit complete with a clear migration plan. The 6-month AI roadmap is on the table.



HOW TO APPLY



Send an
 with the subject line: "Head of AI Application" to 

Your
 must include all five of the following. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.



1. YOUR RESUME 

Attach your updated resume as a PDF or Word document.



2. BRIEF BACKGROUND SUMMARY

In a few sentences, tell us who you are and what you have been doing. What kind of work have you been doing, what type of teams or businesses have you worked with, and what brought you to this kind of role.



3. YOUR AI EXPERIENCE

Give us a specific breakdown of your AI experience. Do not speak in generalities. We want to know:

---> Which AI tools and models you have worked with and at what depth

---> Whether you have built with the Claude API, OpenAI API, Gemini API, or others

---> Whether you have built multi-agent or agentic systems and what they did

---> Any experience with on-premise compute, tunneling, cost routing, or API governance

---> Any experience training or upskilling teams on AI tools and workflows



4. DEMO VIDEO OF YOUR WORK

Submit a screen recording or video walkthrough of an automation or AI project you built yourself. This is the most important part of your application.



We want to see:

---> Something you actually built, not a tutorial you followed

---> How it works end to end, including the prompt logic, data connections, or agent architecture involved

---> The problem it solves and why you built it the way you did

---> Any tradeoffs you made during the build and what you would improve

Upload the video to Google Drive, Loom, YouTube (unlisted), or any shareable link and include it in your
. There is no required length; show us what is necessary to understand what you built.



5. YOUR COMPREHENSIVE 30-DAY PLAN - sent as a pdf or word document

Based on everything described in this job posting, write a detailed plan for what you would do in your first 30 days. This is not a general onboarding plan. We want a specific, technical plan that covers:

---> How you would audit the existing AI systems and what you would look for

---> How you would approach unifying the disconnected systems into one architecture

---> Which optimizations or fixes you would prioritize first and why

---> Any changes you would plan to make to the current model routing, cost structure, or agent architecture

---> How you would approach getting up to speed with the team and the existing codebase



There is no right or wrong answer here. We are evaluating how you think, how specific you get, and whether your plan reflects a genuine understanding of what we have built and what we need.



This role is for someone who has already done this work at this level. Incomplete applications will not be considered. If you are the right person, your application will show it.

 routing and lead capture issues

REQUIREMENTS

PREFERRED

 automation best practices



COMPENSATION

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

• Detail-oriented and organized

• Process-driven and proactive

title: 0% business funding CSM
description:
We're a U.S.-based business credit and capital access company helping established business owners access $50K–$250K+ in 0% APR funding. We work with a selective client base and hold a high standard for client experience throughout the entire funding process.



We're hiring a Client Success Manager to own the client relationship from onboarding through capital deployment and beyond.



What You'll Be Doing

Guiding clients through a structured, phase-based program with clear milestones and deliverables

Managing credit repair workflows — disputing inaccuracies, tracking score progress, and coordinating with our credit repair VA

Keeping clients accountable to action items and timelines without hand-holding

Flagging at-risk clients early and escalating to the team when needed

Maintaining clean CRM records and updating client status consistently

Communicating professionally across 
,
, and video calls





Requirements



1–2+ years of CSM experience specifically at a funding, business credit, or capital access company — this is non-negotiable. We will verify.

Demonstrated experience with credit repair processes (disputes, bureau communications, score monitoring)

Strong written and spoken English

Reliable internet connection and a distraction-free work setup

Comfortable working U.S. business hours (or significant overlap)

Detail-oriented, process-driven, and able to manage multiple active clients simultaneously





What We're Not Looking For



General VA experience rebranded as CSM experience

Anyone who needs to be taught the basics of business credit from scratch



Just so i know you read the whole thing, make sure in your application, you put the subject line as "banana"

title: Social Media Manager — Jewelry Brand Remote | Part-time (20 hrs/week) | $5–$8/hr
description:
**About the Role**



We are a jewelry brand looking for a creative and self-driven Social Media Manager to take full ownership of our social media presence. This is a remote, part-time role (20 hrs/week) open to candidates based in the Philippines.



You will be responsible for planning, creating, and publishing content across all social media platforms — no hand-holding. We need someone who can work independently, think like a brand, and consistently deliver quality content.



All hours are tracked via Time Doctor. You must be comfortable installing and using it before your first shift.



---



**Responsibilities**



- Manage and grow all social media accounts (Instagram, TikTok,
, Pinterest, etc.)

- Independently create content — graphics, captions, Reels/Shorts — aligned with brand aesthetics

- Develop and execute monthly content calendars

- Research trending topics, hashtags, and competitors in the jewelry/fashion space

- Monitor and respond to DMs, comments, and community engagement

- Track analytics and submit monthly performance reports

- Coordinate with influencers or UGC creators when needed

- Stay current with platform algorithm changes and best practices



---



**Requirements**



- 2–3years of social media management experience

- Experience with jewelry, fashion, or lifestyle brands

- Strong visual design skills (Canva, Adobe Photoshop/Illustrator)

- Proficient in creating Reels, TikToks, and YouTube Shorts

- Solid understanding of hashtag strategy and organic reach

- Familiar with analytics tools (Meta Business Suite, Instagram Insights, etc.)

- Experience with content scheduling tools (Later, Buffer, or similar)

- Basic knowledge of product photography and jewelry styling

- Excellent written and spoken English

- Stable internet connection

- Comfortable using Time Doctor for time tracking



---



**Nice to Have**



- Experience with influencer outreach or UGC coordination

- Adobe Premiere or CapCut video editing skills

- Pinterest strategy experience



---



**Job Details**



- Hours: 20 hrs/week

- Pay: $5–$8/hr depending on experience and portfolio

- Time tracking: Time Doctor required during all working hours

- Contract type: Independent contractor

- Start date: ASAP



---



**Hiring Process**



1. Application review — We review your resume, portfolio, and links to accounts you have managed.

2. Sample work — Shortlisted candidates will be given a short sample task (e.g., 2–3 sample posts or a mini content calendar for a jewelry brand).

3. Interview — Candidates who pass the sample work stage will be invited to a short video interview.

4. Offer — Successful candidates will receive an offer with onboarding and Time Doctor setup details.



---



**How to Apply**



Please send the following:

- Brief introduction and summary of your relevant experience (resume)

- Portfolio or links to social media accounts you have managed

- Your availability and expected hourly rate



Applications without a portfolio will not be considered. We are looking for someone who truly understands the jewelry and fashion aesthetic and takes initiative without being asked.

 or Instagram ads.

 Ads

Meta Business Manager

title: Graphic Designer — Apparel and Print
description:
God Ain't Petty But I Am® is a politically bold, unapologetically progressive apparel brand based in Phoenix, AZ. We drop new statement designs multiple times per week and need a designer who can take written statements and turn them into bold, print-ready t-shirt graphics.



What you will do: Receive written statements from the founder, design bold print-ready t-shirt graphics, upload finished designs to Shopify, and coordinate with our operations team on product listings. All designs must be returned the same day they are assigned.



What we need: Proficiency in Adobe Illustrator, Adobe Photoshop, or Kittl. Strong typographic instincts. Our designs are text-forward, and the typography has to hit. Experience with apparel or print design preferred. Reliable same-day turnaround. This is non-negotiable. If you cannot commit to same-day delivery, please do not apply.



Important: We are a human design studio. We do not use AI image generation tools such as Midjourney, Ideogram, or similar platforms. Please do not apply if your process relies on these tools.



Shortlisted candidates will be given a paid test project of 3 t-shirt designs for $20. You will also be required to submit a screen recording of yourself completing the test project using Loom or any screen recording tool. This confirms the work is your own. The test project must be completed on the same day to be considered.



To apply: Send your application with at least 3 examples of apparel or typographic design work. Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.

title: Short Form Video Editor — Daily Same Day
description:
God Ain't Petty, But I Am® is a politically bold, unapologetic apparel brand based in Phoenix, AZ. We create 2+ short form videos daily for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts, and need a fast, reliable editor to receive raw footage and return finished files the same day.



What you will do: Receive raw vertical video footage from the founder daily, add captions, edit for pacing and flow, format finished files for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts, and return all completed videos the same day they are received.



What we need: Proficiency in CapCut or Premiere Pro. Strong experience editing short-form vertical video, 30 to 60 seconds in length. Ability to add accurate captions. Reliable same-day turnaround. This is non-negotiable. All raw footage submitted by 8 am Phoenix time (MST) must be returned by 1 pm Phoenix time. If you cannot commit to this schedule, please do not apply. Consistent daily availability Monday through Saturday.



Shortlisted candidates will be given a paid test project of 2 to 3 short-form video edits for $15. You will also be required to submit a screen recording of yourself completing the edit using Loom or any screen recording tool. This confirms the work is your own. The test project must be completed on the same day to be considered.



To apply: Send your application with at least 3 examples of short-form vertical video edits you have completed. Applications without a portfolio will not be considered.

title: Social Media & Content Producer
description:
About Hair Clubs

Hair Clubs is a B2B SaaS loyalty platform that helps ambitious salon owners retain clients and grow their business without doing any marketing — by turning their clients into the marketing team. We’re building a brand that looks and feels like nothing else in the hair industry. Think run club meets hair. The creative bar is high. The pace is fast. 



The Role

You are the person who can drive content ideas, structure it into a calendar rhythm and turn it into a published, designed, edited, captioned post — without needing three people in the middle. Right now our content pipeline has too many handoffs. We need one person who can collapse that chain for the daily output.



You’re not replacing our Art Director. She sets the brand world, leads campaigns, and produces the high-end creative. You’re the daily engine — the person who keeps the grid alive, the stories running, the comments answered, and the content flowing without bottlenecking anyone else on the team. 





The most important thing: you think about what happens after you post. Every post exists to create a response, and when that response comes, someone needs to be on the other end. That someone is you. Additionally, you think of how we capture people’s information from socials and move it into our database and you’re not afraid to ask the engineers to build an automation to do that.



Content Production (60%)

 • Design social assets in Photoshop — tiles, carousels, EDM graphics, cover tiles, thumbnails

 • Edit short-form video for Reels, Stories, and TikTok (CapCut, Premiere, or DaVinci)

 • Create reel cover tiles independently without needing the Art Director for every post

 • Build and maintain reusable templates for recurring content formats

 • Produce EDM creative (graphic design)

 • Repurpose long-form content (podcast episodes) into short-form clips

 • Work with the Art Director on brand-level creative when needed, but own the daily output independently

 • create copy 



Social Media Management (40%)

 • Own the Hair Clubs, YSS and CWGL  Instagram end-to-end — ideation, creation, posting, engagement

 • Write captions and CTAs that connect into the customer journey

 • Respond to every comment and DM same-day

 • Run Instagram Stories and maintain Highlights

 • Manage the Hair Clubs broadcast channel

 • Schedule and repost existing show content to secondary channels

 • Default to collaboration on all tagged/co-created content

 • Drop a weekly social media report every Friday



Required Tools & Skills

 • Adobe Photoshop — intermediate (layers, masks, text, export for web, template adaptation)

 • Video Editing (CapCut / Premiere / DaVinci) — intermediate (cuts, transitions, text overlays, audio sync, vertical format)

 • Claude — you already use AI in your workflow 

 • Instagram / Meta Business Suite — confident (scheduling, insights, collab posts, stories, highlights)

 • Slack, Notion, Google Workspace — working proficiency



You’ll Thrive Here If

 • You’ve run a brand’s Instagram end-to-end and can show growth, engagement, and creative range

 • Your portfolio shows design and video work, not just strategy decks

 • You’re the kind of person who sees a competitor’s post and immediately thinks “we should do our version of that”

 • You can take a 45-minute podcast and turn it into 5 reels without being given timestamps

 • You treat every post like it’s a conversation starter, not a checkbox

 • You’re comfortable working in a small, fast team where everyone sees everything





Salary negotiable





Take a look at our instagram for creative

title: Virtual Assistant
description:

This is not a creative strategy role.

If you can copy prompts, paste them into AI tools, place outputs into Canva, and deliver results exactly as instructed…

Read on.



?



Your Job:



Take pre-written prompts from our ticket system

Insert them into Higgsfield

Export the generated results

Place them into Canva templates

Deliver the final output back into the ticket



That’s it.



No ideation.

No copywriting.

No strategy decisions.



Everything is fully systemized with SOPs, templates, and examples.



?

?

?

?



Who You Are:

• You don’t overcomplicate simple work

• You take pride in delivering clean, correct outputs

• You’re comfortable doing repetitive tasks efficiently

?

(please include your expected monthly rate in USD)

?

Use subject line:

“AI Prompt Operator – Execution Role”



?

title: Qualified Accountant / Financial Controller with UK experience 3 years plus
description:
Please only apply for this job if you have at least 3 years plus experience working for a UK company



We are a UK Property Company turning over £4 million with assets of £40 million

We need a qualified Accountant to act as a Financial Controller performing a number of internal financial roles. The right person will be familiar with UK Company Accounts and Taxation and have experience working for a UK company in a similar role. 

We operate as a group with 12 different UK Property Companies, a UK Management company and a UK Building Company. All our accounts are in Xero so Xero expertise is essential. We use excel to store our main data so excel expertise is also essential

The main roles will be as follows

1. To prepare cashflow forecasts for the Group Companies and to monitor their performance month by month and to advise the Directors how to ensure we keep in budget

2. To prepare individual budgets for the many building works we carry out and to ensure jobs are carried out on time and on budget

3. To co-ordinate planned expenditure to ensure it is affordable by each individual Property Company

4. To monitor rent allocations into Xero.

5. To monitor expenditure and to ensure that all queries raised by our bookkeeper regarding missing invoices are answered. As part of that role to look at the systems we are using to ensure that monies spent are linked to the relevant invoice. So for example when we pay bills to utility suppliers by direct debit ensuring that the relevant invoice can be found and posted

6. To monitor the activities of our bookkeeper and Accountant in the preparation of monthly accounts and yearly accounts and tax returns. 

7. To advise on tax payments due and ensure funds for payment are available

8. To advise generally on managing our various bank accounts and getting best returns from surplus funds

9. To advise the Directors on all financial matters relating to the group

Key Requirements

1. Experience working with UK companies in a similar role. Work with US or Australian companies will not qualify for this job

2. Formal Accounting qualifications

3. Xero Expertise

4. Excel Expertise

5. Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English

6. Experience advising UK Companies on financial matters and the ability to give practical advice on financial problems that arise from time to time

7. The ability to monitor staff. You will be responsible for a team of 4 people which we are expecting to expand

If this is you please apply with a detailed CV

title: PPC Strategist
description:
Hey! We’re Dilate, a result driven digital marketing agency with an impressive, diverse client list and a world-class, global team.



It’s our mission to do great marketing, for great businesses, ethically and in a way that benefits us all. We want our clients to win, their customers to win, and our employees to win. 



We’ve got pretty ambitious growth plans and we’re looking for a PPC Strategist to join our team.



We are looking for someone with an in depth knowledge of Google Ads &
 ads would be ideal, a keen eye for detail, focused on ROI and strategic thought. Ideally you will have experience running a range of low to high budget CPA based campaigns for businesses on the agency side. 



This opportunity is remote working from home, full-time Monday to Friday 9-5 pm (AWST) as we are an agency located in Perth, Western Australia. 



So, the job...



* Develop and manage PPC campaigns on Google Ads and Meta Ads, ensuring they meet the target KPIs.

* Analyse performance data and adjust strategies to increase campaign efficiency and ROI.

* Understanding of Google Analytics to monitor and report on campaign performance.

* Stay updated with PPC and SEM trends, tools, and best practices to implement in campaigns.

* Provide strategic recommendations on data-driven insights.

* Manage budgets effectively, ensuring all campaigns are delivered within the allocated budget.



Your skills:



* Excellent written and spoken English

* Certified in Google Ads with a deep understanding of paid search, display, and video performance metrics.

* Experience with Google Analytics, capable of interpreting and reporting data to inform decision-making.

* Proven track record in managing successful PPC campaigns, with a strong understanding of optimisation practices for both search and display advertising.

* Good instincts for understanding of conversion rate optimisation & making the most from our websites.

* Ability to propose and write persuasive ad copy if needed.

* Strategic thinker with the ability to generate innovative ideas and translate them into actionable plans.

* Proficiency in managing
/ Instgram Ads, including segmentation, targeting, and ad creation.

* Ability to work independently and as part of a team, with a proactive approach to problem-solving.

* Hungry for knowledge, learning and personal development. 

 * At least 3mbps of stable internet connection (with a backup internet)

* MUST have a mid-level performance desktop or laptop (good for multitasking)

* MUST have a working mic & webcam



This is a huge opportunity to be a part of a fast growing agency.



If you feel that this is the job for you, we would love to hear from you! Please include your resume and cover letter.



Thanks for your time,

The Dilate Digital Team

title: Junior Production Artist
description:
IMPORTANT: We will only process applications of those who will apply through this link





Company and Vision



PlanetArt’s vision is to be the leading seller of personalized and make-on-demand products worldwide. We provide consumers with unmatched tools and content and an unparalleled end-to-end customer experience that result in high-quality, meaningful finished products and memorable celebrations of life events.



The company’s brands include the popular FreePrints and FreePrints Photobooks apps and the industry leading SimplytoImpress card and stationery site, as well as Personal Creations, CafePress and ISeeMe! Visit 
 to learn more about our brands.



We have more than 500 tea
mbers across multiple offices, primarily in Calabasas CA, San Diego CA, Woodridge IL, Minneapolis, MN and Pleasanton, CA. We also have tea
mbers in two company-owned offices in China, as well as in Europe.



Job Overview

PlanetArt is looking for a Junior Production Artist as a contractor to support the company’s Content and Merchandising team across multiple brands. The ideal candidate will be proficient in Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator, excel at following detailed instructions, and possess a sharp eye for detail with strong problem-solving abilities, as this role involves a lot of quality control processes along the way. This is not a creative design role. We’re looking for someone with technical artistic skills to help process imagery and product information for our websites.



This is a remote, work-from-home position.





What You’ll Do

Key Responsibilities

Edit and optimize product imagery for site and app use

Implement revisions and improvements based on feedback.

Maintain image quality through non-destructive editing techniques.

Coordinate image uploads and review/approval workflows.

Configure products and designs by inputting data into template systems

Regularly participate in remote meetings with U.S. tea
mbers to discuss project status and requirements



What You Should Have

Skills, Qualifications, and Requirements

1 year of relevant production experience

Strong English fluency with solid written and verbal communication skills.

Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively, self-manage when needed, communicate task progress, and proactively address challenges within a team environment

Exceptional attention to detail and highly organized.

Eagerness and commitment to quickly learn and master new software platforms, etools, and workflows.

Proven ability to deliver accurate work under tight deadlines.

Positive attitude, self-motivation, and ability to work independently with minimal oversight.

 

What You Can Expect

Working Conditions



Position requires (long-term or seasonal) and fully remote work scheduled from (ex. 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM US Pacific Time)

Position requires regular, continuous use of computer.

Position requires regular sitting and standing.

Position requires regular interaction with tea
mbers through the following methods: phone, WebEx, Slack, or
.

Position requires time and screen tracking via Hubstaff

 

Work from Home Requirements: 



Own computer (Windows 10 or higher or Mac, at least 8GB RAM and at least Intel i5, Ryzen 5 or M1 processor)

Reliable high-speed internet access

Noise-cancelling headset (if applicable)

Private, distraction-free environment (if applicable)

 

Pay Range



Pay Rate: ?40,000 to ?50,000 per month

 



Perks



Paid Time Off

Paid US Holidays

title: Senior Amazon PPC Manager – Remote (Full-Time)
description:
Senior Amazon PPC Manager – Remote (Full-Time)



You live and breathe Amazon Ads. You've scaled accounts from 6 to 7 and 8 figures. You don't just "manage PPC" – you build strategies that crush the competition.



If that sounds like you, keep reading. If not, this role is not for you.



About Us

We are Fox Performance, an Amazon PPC agency founded in 2018. We work mainly with German clients, but also serve international brands across Europe and the USA. Our clients come from every industry you can imagine – supplements, beauty, home & living, pet, electronics, fashion, baby products, and more.



We are obsessed with Amazon. We test everything, read every update, break down every new beta feature, and push our clients to results they didn't think were possible. No autopilot. No "set and forget". No agency BS.

Now we are looking for a Senior Amazon PPC Manager – someone who can take full ownership of a portfolio of accounts, think strategically, and mentor junior tea
mbers along the way.



Who We Are NOT Looking For

Let's be direct. Do not apply if:



You are looking for a short-term gig or just a "bridge job" until something else comes up.

You are working multiple jobs in parallel and trying to squeeze us in on the side. We need your focus. Period.

You don't want to grow, learn, and push yourself every single week.

You want to clock in, clock out and never go beyond the bare minimum.

You see Amazon PPC as "just a job" instead of a craft you want to master.

You can't take direct, honest feedback without taking it personally.



We're not for everyone – and that's okay.

Who We ARE Looking For

We are looking for a true A-Player. Someone who:



Wants to go full throttle and be one of the best in the game.

Treats client accounts like their own business.

Is hungry, curious, and obsessed with getting better.

Wants to grow with us long-term and build a real career.

Brings energy, ideas, and ownership to the table every single day.



If that's you – we'd love to meet you.



What You'll Own



Full Account Ownership: manage a portfolio of premium client accounts end-to-end – from strategy to execution to client communication.

Advertising Strategy: build PPC strategies across Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, Sponsored Display – and where relevant, Amazon DSP and AMC.

Launch & Scale: from new product launches to aggressive scaling of existing winners – you know when to push and when to defend.

Advanced Optimization: bid strategies, placement optimization, SKU-level profitability, TACoS management, dayparting, portfolio structuring – you go way beyond dashboard-level.

Account Audits & Strategic Planning: diagnose weaknesses, identify opportunities, build 30/60/90 day roadmaps for every account.

Client Communication: lead client calls, present results, push back when needed, be the trusted expert clients rely on.

Mentorship: help our Junior PPC Managers and Assistants grow – share your playbook, review their work, develop the next generation.

Process Development: help us build better SOPs, templates and internal tools – your experience shapes how we operate.

Stay Ahead: test new Amazon features, evaluate new tools, and bring fresh ideas to the team.





What You Must Know (Non-Negotiables)

These are the things you absolutely need to have under your belt as a Senior:



Deep mastery of all Sponsored Ads formats: Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands (incl. Video & Brand Store), Sponsored Display (incl. Audiences & Custom Creatives), and how they interact.

Campaign architecture: SKAG, single-keyword, theme-based, performance-based – you know which structure works when and why.

Advanced bidding strategies: dynamic bids, placement modifiers, top-of-search optimization, bid by placement, bid hijacking defense.

Keyword research & targeting: Helium 10 (Cerebro, Magnet, Frankenstein), Brand Analytics, Search Query Performance Report (SQP) – you can pull insights most managers miss.

TACoS vs ACoS: you understand the difference, when to optimize for which, and how to communicate it to clients.

Search Term Reports & Negative Mining: you build clean, performance-focused accounts without keyword cannibalization.

Brand Defense & Conquesting: you protect your client's branded search and attack competitors strategically.

Organic & Paid Synergy: you understand how PPC influences organic ranking (and vice versa) – BSR, indexation, halo effect.

Listing Fundamentals: you know that even the best PPC can't fix a bad listing – and you can identify what needs to change.

Reporting & Storytelling: you don't just deliver numbers – you tell the story behind them.

Bulk File Operations: you can edit, optimize and upload bulk files like a surgeon.

Tool Stack: Helium 10, Brand Analytics, Pacvue / Perpetua / Adtomic / Sellerboard or similar – at least one professional ad management tool you've worked with hands-on.

Bonus (high value): Amazon DSP, AMC (Amazon Marketing Cloud), Vendor Central, international marketplaces (US, UK, DE, FR, IT, ES).



If you read this list and thought "yes, that's me" – continue. If half of these are unfamiliar, this role is not the right fit yet.



What You Bring



4+ years of hands-on Amazon Advertising experience (agency or in-house), ideally managing multiple 6- and 7-figure accounts.

Proven track record of scaling Amazon accounts profitably – with real case studies you can walk us through.

Expert-level data analysis skills – Excel / Google Sheets is your playground; pivot tables, XLOOKUP, conditional logic are second nature.

Strategic mindset – you think in frameworks, not just dashboards.

Excellent English (spoken and written). German is a big plus, but not required.

Strong communication – confident in client calls, clear in writing, direct but professional.

Ownership mentality – fully accountable, no excuses.

Stable internet and a professional remote work setup.





What We Offer

You already know the foundation we offer (100% remote, MacBook, full-time, long-term, competitive USD salary, after one year: 13th month pay, 20 days PTO, paid sick leave, Philippine holidays...).





Ownership & Growth



Clear path to Team Lead / Head of PPC – we are growing, and senior hires are who we promote from.

Voice in strategy – you help shape how the agency operates.

Direct line to leadership – no middle management, no politics.

Conference & event budget – Amazon Unboxed, PPC events, industry conferences – we send you.

Annual learning budget – courses, certifications, books, coaches – you choose.



Time & Space to Do Great Work



Flexible hours – core overlap with German business hours (2–3 hours/day), the rest is yours.

Deep Work culture – no unnecessary meetings, no micromanagement.

Real team culture – weekly team calls, virtual coffee, and annual team events.



If you read the full text start your message with "HI WOLF"
